sections.subtitle: Tottenham Hotspur Form
sections.text: Tottenham Hotspur have shown strong attacking form in their last 5 matches, winning 3, drawing 1, and losing 1, with a notable 3-0 victory over West Ham. They average 2 goals per game and have been first to score in 4 of their last 5 matches, indicating a proactive style at home.

sections.subtitle: Villarreal Form
sections.text: Villarreal have had mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses. Their standout 5-0 win over Girona shows their attacking potential, largely driven by in-form players like Nicolas Pépé and Ayoze Pérez, though their away form has been less consistent.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last encounter in 2010, Villarreal dominated with a 4-1 victory over Tottenham Hotspur. Although historical, this result suggests Villarreal can pose a significant threat if they exploit defensive lapses.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights
sections.text: Tottenham's home matches often see high corner counts (averaging around 6-13 per game) and fewer cards (under 4.5 in 8/10 matches). Villarreal's games also trend under 2.5 goals in 4/5 recent matches, hinting at a potentially tight game despite both teams' attacking capabilities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Overall half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Tottenham Hotspur lead Villarreal 1-0 at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in London. The home side has dominated possession and created more threatening opportunities, reflected in their early goal. Villarreal, on the other hand, have struggled to make an impact in the final third, failing to register a shot on target. Both teams have shown discipline issues with two yellow cards each, which could play a role in the second half. The match remains open, but Tottenham hold a clear advantage going into the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Tottenham Hotspur have been the stronger side in the first half, controlling the ball for a significant portion of the game. Their frequent entries into the final third and penalty area indicate a persistent attacking threat. Defensively, they’ve been solid, winning over half of their duels and aerial battles. Players like Mohammed Kudus and Micky van de Ven have stood out with their contributions in both attack and defense. However, Tottenham will need to capitalize on their dominance to extend their lead, as they’ve missed chances to put the game out of reach.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Villarreal have found themselves on the back foot for much of the first half, with limited time on the ball and fewer entries into dangerous areas. Their inability to test Tottenham’s goalkeeper is a major concern, and they’ve struggled to win duels, particularly in the air. Defensively, they’ve managed to limit the damage to just one goal, with players like Santiago Mouriño and Pape Gueye making key contributions. Villarreal need to find a way to turn possession into meaningful chances if they hope to get back into this game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Tottenham Hotspur set the tone early with consistent attacking pressure in the opening minutes. Villarreal responded with a spell of momentum in the middle of the half, pushing forward with intent but failing to create clear opportunities. As the half progressed, Tottenham regained control with several sustained attacking phases, especially towards the end of the half. The momentum clearly favored the home side by the break, while Villarreal’s attacking efforts dwindled, leaving them with little to show for their occasional pushes forward.
